seo.landingTitle: Plugcut · Plug someone in, take your cut.
seo.landingDescription: Formalize and execute the private commission the person you placed owes you: a percentage of the day rate, over time. Contract, attribution proof, recurring collection, discretion.

contacts.title: Your contacts
contacts.lead: Your private network address book: the people and companies you can place, or who can place you.
contacts.empty: No contacts yet. Add the first person in your network.

import.lead: Pre-fill from a PDF (LinkedIn profile export or CV). You can review and correct everything before saving.
import.error: Could not read this file. Enter the details manually.

errors.generic: An action failed. Please try again.

hero.subtitle: Placed a freelancer, an indie, a company? Plugcut formalizes and executes the private commission they owe you: a percentage of the daily rate, over time. Discreetly, with a real contract.
hero.note: The relationship already exists. Plugcut secures the agreement, not the intro.

items: The project lead placing a freelancer
The baker with a developer buddy
The ex-colleague handing you a client
The consultant opening their address book

how.title: From intro to payout, on a single rail.

steps.title: Create the deal
steps.desc: Who placed whom, at which client. Kept discreet from the end client.

steps.title: Set the commission
steps.desc: A percentage of the day rate, over a duration. For example 10% over 12 months.

steps.title: Two-sided acceptance
steps.desc: Both parties accept the terms. That is your attribution proof, timestamped and sealed.

steps.title: Contract generated
steps.desc: The written agreement between referrer and placed person, ready to sign.

steps.title: Recurring collection
steps.desc: Your cut is computed and tracked against the day rate, month after month.

pillars.title: What Plugcut secures, where a handshake falls short.

items.title: A real contract
items.desc: The written, signed agreement between you and the placed person. A clean frame, not a promise.

items.title: Attribution proof
items.desc: Who introduced whom, timestamped and sealed by fingerprint. Your protection in a dispute.

items.title: Recurring collection
items.desc: Your commission tracked and invoiced against the day rate, month after month. A clear schedule, nothing slips.

items.title: Total discretion
items.desc: The end client sees nothing. The agreement stays strictly between the two of you.

calc.title: What's your intro worth?
calc.subtitle: Estimate your cut over the duration. The real figure follows the days actually billed.
calc.disclaimer: Indicative estimate. The final figure is based on the days actually billed by the placed person.

items.q: Why not just shake hands and send an invoice?
items.a: Because on 10% of a day rate for a year, the referrer fears being forgotten, and the placed person wants a clean, discreet frame. Plugcut sells trust and execution of the deal, not the intro.
The placed person, never the end client. Privately, discreetly.
No. The relationship already exists offline. We formalize and track the agreement. We do not do matching.
Anyone. A project lead, a developer, or someone entirely outside tech who knows the right person.

Plugcut gives you the contract, the attribution proof, and the invoices to frame the commission cleanly between both parties. For the exact tax treatment, check with your accountant: we provide the frame, not the advice.

cta.title: Your network has value. Cash it in.
cta.subtitle: Put your next referral on a clean, discreet, contractual rail.

footer.tagline: The rail for referral commissions, person to person.

legal.demoNote: Plugcut is a demonstration project. This document is provided as an example and has no legal value.

sections.heading: Publisher
sections.body: Plugcut is a personal demonstration project published by an independent developer. No company is registered for this project.

sections.heading: Hosting
sections.body: The demonstration app is hosted on standard cloud infrastructure. In production, the host's identity and contact details would be stated here.

sections.heading: Intellectual property
sections.body: The name, design, and code of Plugcut belong to their author. Any reproduction or reuse without permission is prohibited.

sections.heading: Contact
sections.body: For any question about this project, a contact address would be provided here in a production version.

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.body: Plugcut formalizes and tracks referral commission agreements made between individuals. The platform does not connect strangers and does not take part in the relationship with the end client.

sections.heading: Account
sections.body: You are responsible for keeping your credentials confidential and for the actions taken from your account.

sections.heading: Role of the platform
sections.body: Plugcut provides a frame to record an agreement, prove attribution, and track commissions. Plugcut is not a party to the contract made between the referrer and the placed person.

sections.heading: Commissions
sections.body: The rate, duration, and amount of the commission are set freely by the parties. The estimates shown are indicative and computed from the information you enter.

sections.heading: Liability
sections.body: As a demonstration project, the service is provided without warranty. The author cannot be held liable for any damage related to its use.

sections.heading: Personal data
sections.body: The processing of personal data is described in the privacy policy.

sections.heading: Data collected
sections.body: Plugcut collects your account email address and the information about the deals you create (client reference, commission terms).

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.body: This data is used only to provide the service: authentication, display, and tracking of your agreements.

sections.heading: Retention
sections.body: Data is kept for the duration of your use of the service. In production, specific retention periods would be defined.

sections.heading: Sharing
sections.body: No data is sold. The end client has no access to the agreements. Data is shared only with the technical providers needed to run the service.

sections.heading: Your rights
sections.body: You can request access to, correction of, or deletion of your data. In a production version, a dedicated contact channel would be provided.

sections.heading: Security
sections.body: Passwords are stored as a hash (bcrypt) and access to the API is protected by signed tokens.
